title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol Files Supreme Court Appeal Against Seven-Year Prison Sentence
summary: Former President Yoon Suk-yeol and his wife Kim Keon-hee filed separate appeals with the Supreme Court challenging increased appellate court sentences for obstruction of arrest and bribery charges.
contents:
## Yoon and Wife Challenge Increased Prison Sentences

### Appeals Filed
Former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol filed an appeal with the Supreme Court on Thursday challenging his seven-year prison sentence. His wife, Kim Keon-hee, separately appealed her four-year sentence. Both sentences represented increases from their original trials.

### Sentencing Details
The appellate court sentenced Yoon to seven years in prison on Wednesday for charges including "obstruction of arrest." Kim received a four-year sentence on Tuesday for bribery charges. Both sentences exceeded the original trial penalties.

### Political Context
Yoon's legal troubles stem from his controversial December 2024 martial law declaration, which triggered a constitutional crisis, his impeachment, and eventually criminal prosecution. The case continues to divide South Korean society.

### Legal Process
The Supreme Court appeals represent the final stage of the judicial process. South Korea's three-tier court system allows for appellate review followed by Supreme Court consideration.

### Domestic Impact
The ongoing legal proceedings against the former president maintain political uncertainty in South Korea at a time when the country faces significant security challenges on the Korean Peninsula.
